description_long: This is a goblet-shaped hand drum with an octagonal top, reminiscent of a djembe or darbuka, featuring a taut, slightly weathered drumhead. The drum body is adorned with colorful, geometric tribal patterns in earthy tones, including green, brown, purple, and white, which wrap around the upper and lower sections. Black, crisscrossing tension ropes or bands are visible around the upper body, securing the drumhead and adding structural detail. The base is flared and faceted, decorated with additional painted motifs, and appears to be made of wood or a similar material. The drumhead surface is matte and shows subtle signs of use, while the body finish is more vibrant and decorative. No handles, lids, or overt signage are present, and the drum is designed to be played by hand, likely in outdoor or community settings.
description: A goblet-shaped hand drum with an octagonal, weathered drumhead and colorful, geometric tribal patterns on a wooden body, secured by black tension bands.
description_view_0: Front view showing the octagonal drumhead, black tension bands, and the most detailed section of the painted geometric patterns on the upper and lower body.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the continuous tribal patterns and the faceted, flared base, with the tension bands more prominent on the left.
description_view_2: Rear angled view displaying the green and brown painted motifs and a clear view of the drum's flared base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the drumhead and tension bands visible, and a slightly different arrangement of the painted patterns on the body.
